question: [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / take] Can an unprivileged taker deploy a loan token whose code self-destructs after market creation under the precondition that the market is live with active positions, then call take() triggering SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(offer.market.loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) which reverts with NoCode() because token.code.length == 0, violating the invariant that valid take() calls must always be executable for any market with live positions, causing scoped impact: permanent DoS of take() for all participants in that market at zero ongoing cost? Proof idea: deploy a self-destructable ERC20 as loanToken, create market, self-destruct token, call take() and assert NoCode() revert.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / repay] Can an unprivileged borrower use a loan token that implements address-based blacklisting under the precondition that the borrower holds debt in a market and the token owner blacklists the borrower's address, then call repay(market, units, onBehalf, address(0), '') triggering SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(market.loanToken, msg.sender, address(this), units) which reverts because the token blacklists msg.sender, violating the invariant that a borrower must always be able to repay their own debt, causing scoped impact: permanent DoS of repay() for the blacklisted borrower, forcing liquidation or permanent bad debt? Proof idea: deploy a blacklistable ERC20, create market, open debt, blacklist borrower, call repay() and assert revert.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: safeTransfer / withdraw] Can an unprivileged attacker set a contract as the receiver of withdraw() under the precondition that the receiver contract's fallback or ERC20 receive hook unconditionally reverts, then call withdraw(market, units, onBehalf, maliciousReceiver) triggering SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(market.loanToken, receiver, units) which propagates the revert via assembly, violating the invariant that a lender must always be able to withdraw their credit, causing scoped impact: permanent DoS of withdraw() for any position whose receiver is a reverting contract? Proof idea: deploy a reverting receiver, call withdraw() with it as receiver, assert revert propagation.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: safeTransfer / liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator pass a reverting contract as receiver in liquidate() under the precondition that a borrower is unhealthy and the collateral token reverts on transfer to the receiver address, then call liquidate(market, collateralIndex, seizedAssets, 0, borrower, false, revertingReceiver, address(0), '') triggering SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, seizedAssets) at line 696 which reverts, violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: DoS of liquidate() for any liquidator using a reverting receiver, blocking bad-debt resolution? Proof idea: deploy a reverting receiver, make position unhealthy, call liquidate() with it and assert revert.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: safeTransfer / claimSettlementFee] Can an unprivileged market creator deploy a loan token that reverts on transfer to the feeClaimer's designated receiver under the precondition that claimableSettlementFee[token] > 0 and the token conditionally reverts for the receiver address, then call claimSettlementFee(token, amount, receiver) triggering SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(token, receiver, amount) which reverts, violating the invariant that accrued protocol fees must always be claimable, causing scoped impact: permanent DoS of claimSettlementFee() for that token, locking protocol revenue? Proof idea: deploy a token that reverts on transfer to a specific address, accumulate fees, call claimSettlementFee() and assert revert.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: safeTransfer / claimContinuousFee] Can an unprivileged market creator deploy a loan token that reverts on transfer to the feeClaimer's receiver under the precondition that marketState[id].continuousFeeCredit > 0 and the token conditionally reverts for the receiver, then call claimContinuousFee(market, amount, receiver) triggering SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(market.loanToken, receiver, amount) which reverts, violating the invariant that accrued continuous fees must always be claimable, causing scoped impact: permanent DoS of claimContinuousFee() for that market, locking protocol revenue? [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / take (large returndata bomb)] Can an unprivileged attacker deploy a loan token whose transferFrom() returns a very large bytes payload on revert under the precondition that the token is used as loanToken in a market, then call take() triggering SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom which executes assembly { revert(add(returndata, 0x20), mload(returndata)) } propagating the full large returndata, violating the invariant that take() must not be griefable via gas exhaustion, causing scoped impact: OOG-based DoS of take() for any caller with insufficient gas, achievable at low attacker cost by crafting a token with large revert payload? [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: safeTransfer / liquidate (collateral token self-destructs)] Can an unprivileged market creator deploy a collateral token that self-destructs after market creation under the precondition that a borrower has supplied that collateral and becomes unhealthy, then call liquidate() triggering SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, seizedAssets) which reverts with NoCode(), violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: permanent DoS of liquidate() for that collateral, preventing bad-debt resolution and freezing lender funds? [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / take (zero-value transfer revert)] Can an unprivileged taker use a loan token that reverts on zero-value transferFrom under the precondition that settlement fee equals the full offer price so buyerAssets - sellerAssets = 0, then call take() triggering SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), 0) which reverts because the token rejects zero-value transfers, violating the invariant that take() must succeed for valid offer parameters, causing scoped impact: DoS of take() for any market where settlement fee equals offer price and the loan token rejects zero-value transfers? [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / take (fee-on-transfer loan token accounting desync)] Can an unprivileged market creator deploy a fee-on-transfer loan token under the precondition that the token charges a percentage fee on every transferFrom, then call take() triggering two SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom calls at lines 455-456 where the protocol receives less than buyerAssets - sellerAssets and the receiver receives less than sellerAssets, violating the invariant that ERC20 transfer deltas must match accounting deltas and that marketState.withdrawable must equal actual withdrawable loan token balance, causing scoped impact: protocol insolvency where lenders cannot withdraw their full credit because actual token balance is less than accounting? Proof idea: deploy a 1% fee-on-transfer token, execute multiple takes, assert IERC20(loanToken).balanceOf(midnight) < marketState.withdrawable.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / repay (fee-on-transfer loan token accounting desync)] Can an unprivileged market creator deploy a fee-on-transfer loan token under the precondition that the token charges a fee on transferFrom, then call repay() triggering SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(market.loanToken, payer, address(this), units) where the protocol receives less than units but marketState.withdrawable is increased by the full units amount, violating the invariant that marketState.withdrawable must not exceed actual token balance, causing scoped impact: insolvency where later withdraw() calls fail because actual balance is insufficient? [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / supplyCollateral (fee-on-transfer collateral token)] Can an unprivileged market creator deploy a fee-on-transfer collateral token under the precondition that the token charges a fee on transferFrom, then call supplyCollateral() triggering SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(collateralToken, msg.sender, address(this), assets) where the protocol receives less than assets but position.collateral[collateralIndex] is increased by the full assets amount, violating the invariant that position.collateral must not exceed actual token balance held by the protocol, causing scoped impact: insolvency where withdrawCollateral() or liquidate() fails because actual collateral balance is insufficient? [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: safeTransfer / withdraw (fee-on-transfer loan token)] Can an unprivileged market creator deploy a fee-on-transfer loan token under the precondition that the token charges a fee on transfer and lenders have accumulated credit, then call withdraw() triggering SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(market.loanToken, receiver, units) where the receiver receives less than units but position.credit is reduced by the full units amount, violating the invariant that a lender's received assets must equal their withdrawn credit units, causing scoped impact: lenders permanently lose a portion of their credit on every withdrawal due to fee-on-transfer? Proof idea: deploy a fee-on-transfer token, withdraw credit, assert receiver balance < withdrawn units.
